id: Een cyberaanval kan grote gevolgen hebben__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0012
text: Alertness ebbs away. Although ABN AMRO’s research shows that organisations are more aware of cyber risks after an attack, there is no guarantee that this alertness will be lasting after a successful attack, says Arwi van der Sluijs, Managing Director of cybersecurity service provider NFIR. “Half a year after an attack we get a call asking whether the measures ‘really have to be that strict’.” NFIR focuses specifically on the upper end of small and medium-sized enterprises, healthcare institutions, municipalities and provincial institutions.

metadata.chunk_id: Een cyberaanval kan grote gevolgen hebben__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0012
metadata.source_slug: Een cyberaanval kan grote gevolgen hebben
metadata.source_title: A cyberattack can have major consequences
metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Cybersecurity, Digitalisering, Sociaal ondernemen
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: Business operations grinding to a halt, customers walking away; a cyberattack can severely damage a company. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: Alertness ebbs away. Although ABN AMRO’s research shows that organisations are more aware of cyber risks after an attack, there is no guarantee that this alertness will be lasting after a successful attack, says Arwi van der Sluijs, Managing Director of cybersecurity service provider NFIR. “Half a year after an attack we get a call asking whether the measures ‘really have to be that strict’.” NFIR focuses specifically on the upper end of small and medium-sized enterprises, healthcare institutions, municipalities and provincial institutions. In the Netherlands, median costs due to cyberattacks in 2023 were around 21,000 dollars per company, insurer [Hiscox](https://www.hiscoxgroup.com/sites/group/files/documents/2023-10/Hiscox-Cyber-Readiness-Report-2023.pdf) reports. This puts our country second worldwide in terms of highest financial damage from cybercrime, behind the United Kingdom and just ahead of the United States. Damage from cyberattacks takes many forms. Insurer Hiscox sheds light in its annual [Cyber Readiness Report](https://www.hiscoxgroup.com/sites/group/files/documents/2023-10/Hiscox-Cyber-Readiness-Report-2023.pdf) on the different negative consequences companies experienced due to a cyberattack. Most reported in 2023 were costs for notifying customers (31 per cent of the affected companies), followed by partner data leaks (26 per cent). Strikingly, the latter figure increased by as much as four percentage points compared with the previous year — a confirmation that cyber risks are not limited to a company’s own operations. At 16 per cent of companies, such incidents even led to the loss of partners. A quarter of organisations reported negative effects on the brand or corporate reputation; at around a fifth, customers walked away, or they experienced difficulties attracting new customers. Many companies also saw their performance worsen (24 per cent); at 21 per cent, there was even a threat to the organisation’s viability. Measures to reduce risks. Entrepreneurs should protect themselves against cyberattacks.

id: Een cyberaanval kan grote gevolgen hebben__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0026
text: First, carry out a risk analysis to map which matters are crucial for keeping the business running and maintaining service levels. Second, take measures such as encouraging safe behaviour among employees, recording who owns specific data and discussing cyber risks with partners and suppliers.
